JavaScript Core Concepts: The Full Series Index (and Why I Wrote It)

This JavaScript Core Concepts series is based on Hexschool's online video course. I'd actually already gone through the "weird parts" of JavaScript before, but here's the thing — every instructor has their own way of explaining things. So I figured I'd start taking notes and learn it all over again.
This is mainly me following along with Hexschool's online video course "
" (in Mandarin). If you want to deepen your JavaScript fundamentals, I'd recommend checking it out.

One nice thing — the course actually encourages students to turn the material into written articles (you can't export the video content directly, but screenshots are fine). Writing it up has real benefits: it cements the knowledge, and it gives you something to quickly look up later when you need it. So it's well worth doing.
Every article below is written in Mandarin. The links point to the original Chinese posts.

I'd already gone through the weird parts of JavaScript before, but every instructor explains things their own way — so I figured I'd take notes and learn it again from scratch.
